items.q: Is a reflex action controlled by conscious thought?
items.correct: No - a reflex is processed through a reflex arc in the spinal cord, giving a fast automatic response without a conscious decision.
items.misconception: You decide to carry out a reflex action using your brain.
items.random_wrong: Reflexes are controlled by hormones released into the blood.
items.why: Students assume all actions are consciously controlled, missing the automatic reflex arc.

items.q: Why does a shoot bend toward light?
items.correct: Auxin gathers on the shaded side and makes the cells there elongate more, bending the shoot toward the light.
items.misconception: The shoot bends because light physically pushes it over.
items.random_wrong: The shoot bends because the lit side grows faster.
items.why: Students reverse which side elongates or imagine light moving the plant directly.

items.q: How do hormonal and nervous responses differ?
items.correct: Nervous responses are fast, short-lived and use electrical impulses; hormonal responses are slower, longer-lasting and travel in the blood.
items.misconception: Hormones travel along nerves to reach their targets.
items.random_wrong: Nervous and hormonal responses act at exactly the same speed.
items.why: Students confuse the two coordination systems and their transport routes.

items.q: The correct order of neurones in a reflex arc is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: sensory, relay, motor. “motor, relay, sensory” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: The chemical released at a synapse to pass the impulse on is a:
items.why: Correct answer: neurotransmitter. “hormone” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A plant shoot bends towards light because auxin:
items.correct: gathers on the shaded side and makes those cells elongate
items.misconception: is destroyed on the shaded side
items.random_wrong: stops all growth on the lit side
items.why: It's gathers on the shaded side and makes those cells elongate. “is destroyed on the shaded side” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Hormonal responses differ from nervous responses because hormones:
items.correct: are carried in the blood and act more slowly
items.why: Correct answer: are carried in the blood and act more slowly. “travel along nerve fibres” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
